# mushi-kit

mushi-kit is a generic, backward-compatible learning layer (not a protocol) that complements MCP by reducing redundant API calls through intelligent caching and crystallization—positioning it against the community's stdio vs HTTP debate. The generics-based architecture supports custom action types while recent research (semantic caching, agentic plan caching) validates the core value prop: 40-60% token reduction and 50% cost savings. Dogfooding surfaced real bugs (content-encoding), demonstrating the approach's practical value for enterprise agent cost optimization.
